examples.title: Algorithm: Cookie-Based Session Management
examples.description: Handling user sessions using cookies in servlets
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ManageSession
BEGIN
 Receive client request
 Extract cookies from request headers
 IF 'userSession' cookie exists THEN
 Validate session ID
 Retrieve user preferences
 ELSE
 Generate new session ID
 Create Cookie object
 Set maxAge and path
 Add cookie to response
 END IF
 Process request
 Send response with updated cookies
END
examples.explanation:
1. Checks for existing session cookie
2. Creates new session if missing
3. Maintains state across HTTP requests
4. Implements cookie properties for persistence

examples.title: JSP Variable Scopes Example
examples.description: Demonstrates declaration and usage of different variable types in JSP
examples.code:
<%! // Declaration (instance variable) int globalCount = 0; %> <% // Scriptlet (local variable) int localCount = 0; String userType = (String) session.getAttribute("userRole"); globalCount++; localCount++; %>

Global: <%= globalCount %>

Local: <%= localCount %>

Role: <%= userType %>
examples.explanation:
1. <%! %> declares application-wide variables
2. <% %> creates request-scoped variables
3. Accesses session implicit object
4. Demonstrates JSP expression syntax

examples.title: Servlet Parameter Handling
examples.description: Reading initialization and request parameters in servlets
examples.explanation:
1. Reads init parameter from web.xml
2. Processes request parameter
3. Uses ternary operator for default value
4. Forwards request to JSP

examples.title: JSP Implicit Objects Usage
examples.description: Accessing built-in request and session objects in JSP
examples.code:
<% // Access request parameters String productId = request.getParameter("pid"); // Set session attribute if (productId != null) { session.setAttribute("lastViewed", productId); } // Application context parameter String maxResults = application.getInitParameter("maxSearchResults"); %>
Last Viewed: <%= session.getAttribute("lastViewed") %>
Max Results: <%= maxResults %>
examples.explanation:
1. Uses request implicit object
2. Manages session attributes
3. Accesses application context parameters
4. Demonstrates JSP scriptlet syntax
